primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, en comptes consolidés, de l’éligibilité et du modèle de comptabilité de couverture applicable à la composante change d’un dividende intragroupe ou de la créance intragroupe correspondante.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the required precedence rule strictly: IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge accounting eligibility and model questions, so IFRS 9 governs this hedge accounting issue and IAS 39 is discarded as overlapping lower-precedence guidance.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 directly addresses hedged items in consolidated financial statements, intragroup foreign currency exceptions, hedge qualification and the three named hedge accounting models.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern hedged-item eligibility in consolidation, the intragroup foreign currency exceptions, hedge documentation and the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain when intragroup monetary foreign exchange differences survive consolidation and how they are recognised, which constrains the application of IFRS 9 intragroup hedge accounting.
This Interpretation clarifies the net investment hedge model, including the nature of the hedged foreign currency risk, group location of the hedging instrument and recycling on disposal.

peripheral_authority.reason: These disclosure requirements relate to hedge accounting presentation and note disclosure, not to identifying the peer accounting approaches.

treatment_families.family: Named hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk exposures in consolidated financial statements
Intragroup foreign currency hedged-item eligibility in consolidation
Specific net investment hedge model within group foreign currency exposures

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on recognised assets or liabilities, which is relevant once a corresponding receivable has been recognised.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows, including foreign currency risk and highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model for qualifying foreign currency exposures within a group.
